rag_rules: Fix unresolved identifiers in AGREE contracts by ensuring all referenced features or helpers are visible in the same annex or properly declared.
Place component type contracts in valid component type scopes, avoiding implementation-level bindings in type contracts.
generic_rules: Use only visible AADL features or declared helpers in AGREE annex expressions to avoid type errors.
Ensure that range assumptions and guarantees are placed in component type scopes with concrete bounds or named constants.
Validate that all identifiers in AGREE contracts are resolvable within the same annex or are valid AADL features.
Avoid referencing subcomponent ports directly in component type contracts unless represented through valid variables or outputs.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE annex expressions must reference only visible identifiers or declared helpers within the same annex scope.
Component type contracts should define input assumptions and output guarantees using valid feature names or constants.
anti_patterns: Do not reference identifiers or helpers that are not visible in the same AGREE annex or AADL scope.
Do not place implementation-level bindings in component type contract scopes.
irrelevant_topics: type scope range assumption and guarantee
